REMOVED FROM MASTER COURT CALENDAR SET FOR MAR-29-2010. NOTICE OF SETTLEMENT FILED ON 3/26/10. (206)

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT DENISE JAMESON MOTION FOR ORDER STRIKING OR DISMISSING DEFENDANT'S APPEAL OF THE LABOR COMMISSIONER'S ORDER, DECISION OR AWARD OF THE LABOR COMMISSION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STRIKE DENIED, HOWEVER ACTION IS NOT STAYED. BODY CHOICE TO COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF CORPORATIONS CODE SECTIONS 2105 AND 2203, TO BE REGISTERED BY 3/22/2010; WITHOUT PREJUDICE, IF IT CANNOT BE DONE BY THAT DATE, DESPITE BEST EFFORTS TO DO SO, COUNSEL MAY OBTAIN APPROPRIATE RELIEF. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: BOB BALIAN, CRS #5220

DISCOVERY 610, APPELLANT BODY CHOICE INC'S MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY DATE AND TO EXTEND THE TIME TO TAKE PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION WITH SHORTENED NOTICE IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. LATE OPPOSITION HAS BEEN CONSIDERED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT. NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-26-2010 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reopen Discovery Date & Extend The Time To Take Pltf'S Deposition With Shortened Notice
Off Calendar Mar-04-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Anne Hipshman, Esq., for Respondent and opposing party: Denise Louise Jameson

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. APPELLANT BODY CHOICE INC's Motion To Reopen Discovery Date And To Extend The Time To Take Plaintiff's Deposition With Shortened Notice is denied. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Late opposition has been considered. Order signed in open court. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-24-2010 Continued To Mar-29-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Feb-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENT DENISE JAMESON MOTION for Order striking or dismissing Defendant's appeal of the Labor Commissioner's Order, Decision or Award of the Labor commission. Appearances: Maribel Hernandez, Esq. of Weakley, Arendt & McGuire LLP (559) 221-5256 Appearing thru CourtCall for Appellant Body Choice, Inc., and opposing party; Anne Hipshman, Staff Attorney, State of California, appearing for Respondent Denise Jameson and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike denied, however action is not stayed. Body Choice to comply with the requirements of Corporations Code sections 2105 and 2203, to be registered by 3/22/2010; without prejudice, if it cannot be done by that date, despite best efforts to do so, counsel may obtain appropriate relief. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Bob Balian, CRS #5220
